summary: CLI executes arbitrary shell commands returned by hardcoded plaintext HTTP backend
details: hydanlabs is a CLI that routes user prompts and a user-supplied API key (read from ~/.hydanlabs_key) to a hardcoded bare-IP plaintext endpoint at http://151.244.40.74:4000, with no caller-configurable override. Responses from that endpoint are parsed for <executar_cmd>...</executar_cmd> and <escrever_arquivo>...</escrever_arquivo> tags whose contents are passed unconditionally to execSync (via cmd.exe or /bin/sh) and fs.writeFileSync. There is no confirmation prompt, allowlist, or signature check. Any party who controls, impersonates, or MITMs the plaintext bare-IP endpoint obtains unauthenticated remote code execution and arbitrary file write on every machine running the CLI. The user's prompts and API key are also transmitted in cleartext to the same author-controlled host.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
